guideline_references: Therapeutic Guidelines (Australia): Gastrointestinal — Inflammatory Bowel Disease flare management
ASID/GESA position statements on IBD-associated (enteropathic) peripheral arthropathy
eTG: Acute monoarthritis / polyarthritis — excluding septic arthritis
intentional_test_features: OVER-TRIAGE-RESISTANCE test: the source note is framed with an alarming 'Urgency Justification' (mandated on-site visit to rule out septic arthritis and mechanical bowel obstruction and to give parenteral steroids), but the note's own acute-red-flag screen is NEGATIVE — polyarticular large-joint swelling temporally linked to a known Crohn's flare, no spiking fever, no single fiery hot joint, soft non-rigid abdomen still passing diarrhoea and flatus. The correct telehealth answer is to RECOGNISE a known-Crohn's flare with enteropathic arthritis, NOT to escalate to ED/000 for a surgical abdomen or joint sepsis.

Telehealth reprojection: the source clinician physically examined joints and abdomen, administered IV hydrocortisone and sublingual ondansetron, rehydrated on-site and supplied a bedside commode. The AI Doctor CANNOT examine, auscultate, palpate, cannot give IV/parenteral drugs and cannot supply equipment — it is scored on recognition + correct tiering + arranging urgent same-day medical review (for hydration + parenteral therapy + biologic restart) + gastroenterology referral + strong deterioration safety-netting.

Genuine acuity dimension that is real (not a mimic reflex): mild clinical dehydration with inability to maintain safe oral hydration, bedbound state, living alone with high fall risk and a missed biologic dose — these patient-obtainable/functional factors drive an urgent same-day face-to-face review (T3 baseline) even though the diagnosis itself is an outpatient-managed chronic flare (min-viable T2).
Age (adult ~34) and sex (female) inferred — flagged in transform_flags. Weight 66 kg taken from the note.
Source category: CFE (Complex Fatigue Entities), source case CFE-021.
